violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by rack. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Rack was relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Raw animal foods were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Utensils were brought to 3-bay sink to be sanitized. 3-bay sink poster provided. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Employee filled soap dispenser at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

Raw animal foods (chicken/beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food was relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC started date marking at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

Container of chicken was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Chicken was relocated to prep table. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024
Knives were observed stuck between prep units. Knives were removed and placed in dirty dish bucket. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of various shelving/equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the PIC, Rice was placed hot in the refrigerator in colanders greater than four inches deep. Food Products set for cooling are to be placed in shallow pans no greater than 4" deep for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

At the time of inspection, Reduced Oxygen Packaged Crab was observed to be thawing in the refrigerator at 40 degrees Fahrenheit. REDUCED OXYGEN PACKAGED SEAFOOD labeled KEEP FROZEN shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. Based on the temperature observed (above 37 degrees Fahrenheit), the Crab was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2023

Plastic tubs used for food storage were observed not stored properly. Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are to be stored inverted. Plastic tubs were properly stored at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023

The following equipment non-food contact surfaces were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration gaskets - Shelving surfaces - Fan shrouds to fan units in the cookline area New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed to be in poor repair... - Floor tiles in the kitchen were observed to be cracked/missing exposing the cement subfloor - Coving along the wall(s) and the walk-in cooler were observed to be torn/missing The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
The Mop Sink Room was observed to have an abundance of items/clutter. Establishment is to be maintained to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023

The hood vent system over the cookline was observed to have an accumulation of grease. The hood vents are to be cleaned as often as needed to keep them clean and prevent them from becoming a source of contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2023
